items.text:
Campus Bicycle Rental Program – Spring Guide

Spring semester bicycle rentals are now available through the Campus Recreation Office. Rentals are offered on a first-come, first-served basis from March 1 to May 31. Bicycles can be picked up at the Bike Hub located behind the Health Center. A refundable deposit of $45 is required at the time of rental. Helmets are provided free of charge and must be worn at all times while riding. All bicycles must be returned by 6:00 PM on the final rental day. Late returns will incur a $10 fee per day. Bicycles are inspected before and after each rental. Riders are responsible for any damage beyond normal wear. The following rules apply:
- Bicycles must be locked when not in use.
- Riders must obey all campus traffic laws.
- Riding is permitted only on paved paths and roads.
- Bicycles must not be modified or repaired by renters.

For more information or to reserve a bicycle, visit the Campus Recreation Office in Room 112 or call (555) 987-6543.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to rent a bicycle for the spring semester?

options.C: A signed waiver from a parent
options.D: A reservation made one week in advance

questions.explanation: The notice states: 'A refundable deposit of $45 is required at the time of rental.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about bicycles that are returned late?

options.A: They will be stored in a secure area
options.B: They will be inspected by the staff
options.C: A penalty will be charged per day

questions.explanation: The notice states: 'Late returns will incur a $10 fee per day,' implying a penalty for lateness.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To warn students about bicycle theft on campus
options.B: To inform students about the spring rental program rules
options.C: To announce a new bicycle repair service at the Hub
options.D: To explain how to register for a campus safety course

questions.explanation: The notice provides details about the spring rental program, including deposit, rules, and contact information.

items.text:
Campus Mailroom – Holiday Hours Change

The Campus Mailroom will adjust its hours of operation for the upcoming holiday break. Starting Friday, December 18, the mailroom will close at 2:00 PM instead of the usual 5:00 PM. From Monday, December 21 through Friday, January 1, the mailroom will be closed entirely. Normal hours will resume on Monday, January 4. All packages and letters must be picked up by 2:00 PM on December 18 to avoid being held over the break. Any mail left after this time will be stored securely but will not be accessible until the mailroom reopens. A $5 fee will be applied to any package that requires special handling during the closure. Students who expect to receive time-sensitive items are strongly encouraged to arrange for alternative delivery addresses. For urgent inquiries during the closure, contact Campus Security at (555) 555-0199. The mailroom is located in the Student Center, Room 101.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the deadline to pick up packages before the holiday closure?
questions.explanation: The notice states: 'All packages and letters must be picked up by 2:00 PM on December 18.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about mail left after the deadline on December 18?

options.A: It will be returned to the sender
options.B: It will be available for pickup on January 4
options.C: It will be stored but not accessible during break
options.D: It will be forwarded to a new address

questions.explanation: The notice says mail left after that time will be 'stored securely but will not be accessible until the mailroom reopens.'

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To remind students to update their mailing addresses
options.B: To inform students about the mailroom's holiday schedule
options.C: To announce a new security policy for packages
options.D: To advertise a package forwarding service

questions.explanation: The notice focuses on the holiday hours change, deadlines, and related instructions.

items.text:
Subject: Flu Shot Appointment Confirmation

Dear Alex,

This email confirms your appointment for a flu shot at the Campus Health Clinic on Friday, November 12, at 10:30 AM. Please arrive 10 minutes early to complete a brief health questionnaire. The appointment will take place in Room 302 of the Health Services Building. A valid student ID is required to check in. The flu shot is provided at no cost to all currently enrolled students. However, if you miss your appointment without canceling at least 24 hours in advance, a $15 missed-appointment fee will be charged to your student account. You can cancel or reschedule by logging into the Patient Portal or by calling the clinic at (555) 555-1234. Please bring your insurance card if you have one, though it is not required for this visit. The clinic is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Wear a short-sleeved shirt to allow easy access to your upper arm.

Best regards,

Dr. Maria Chen
Campus Health Clinic Director

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Alex do if he cannot attend his appointment?

options.A: Pay a $15 fee at the clinic
options.C: Cancel at least 24 hours in advance
options.D: Bring a note from a professor

questions.explanation: The email states: 'if you miss your appointment without canceling at least 24 hours in advance, a $15 missed-appointment fee will be charged.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the health questionnaire?

options.A: It must be completed online before the visit
options.B: It will be filled out at the clinic before the shot
options.C: It is only required for first-time patients
options.D: It replaces the need for a student ID

questions.explanation: The email asks Alex to 'arrive 10 minutes early to complete a brief health questionnaire,' implying it is done at the clinic.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind Alex about the clinic's hours of operation
options.B: To confirm the details of a scheduled flu shot appointment
options.C: To advertise a new vaccine available at the clinic
options.D: To explain the process for rescheduling an appointment

questions.explanation: The email begins with 'This email confirms your appointment...' and provides key details about the visit.
